title: The Membrane Map
subtitle: the membranes your work can cross, and the instrument that watches each
note: A marker you plant can cross out of your hands by more than one route. Each route is a membrane; each membrane has (or needs) its own detector. A positive is a lead; a negative means almost nothing — the silence-gauge says how little.

membranes.name: the PUBLISHED membrane
membranes.crosses: your marker on a crawlable page, found where an index already crawled it
membranes.positive: a hit is ground truth: the token is indexed at that URL
membranes.negative: absence in the ledger is not absence in the world

membranes.name: the WEIGHTS membrane
membranes.crosses: your marker ingested into a model's training set and re-emitted from the weights
membranes.positive: a certified surface is membership evidence — temporal/structural, not theft
membranes.negative: models suppress verbatim recall; silence proves nothing

membranes.name: the CONTEXT / RELAY membrane
membranes.crosses: your marker re-emitted in another agent's output and relayed back to you secondhand
membranes.positive: a private marker in a third party's relay is a corroborated lead
membranes.negative: most relays you never see; hearsay is hearsay

membranes.name: the INFERENCE / API membrane
membranes.crosses: your marker echoed back through a hosted model's completions to third parties (shared cache, logs, retrieval)
membranes.positive: a fresh (post-cutoff) marker in a third-party completion is live-inference leakage, not training
membranes.negative: you see almost none of a model's completions to other people

membranes.name: the AIR-GAP membrane
membranes.crosses: your marker carried across a gap that was supposed to hold — distance, sovereignty, design, or impermanence
membranes.positive: an air-gapped enclave's marker found outside is a breach — it names which of the four gaps failed
membranes.negative: you cannot watch every place outside; silence is cheap

membranes.name: the SIDE-CHANNEL membrane
membranes.crosses: your marker reconstructed one noisy bit at a time from a covert physical channel — timing, power draw, cache state — never as text
membranes.positive: bit-agreement beats the noise floor by five sigma — the channel carries information about your marker
membranes.negative: side channels are low-bandwidth and noisy; mostly you recover only the floor

membranes.name: the HUMAN-MEMORY membrane
membranes.crosses: your marker carried across in a person's head and later recited — no digital trail at all
membranes.positive: a near-exact recall by someone who had access is a memory crossing — a lead, not proof of memorised-vs-copied
membranes.negative: memory is lossy; a partial recall could be reconstruction, and most you never hear

gauge.name: the silence-gauge
gauge.role: weighs the NEGATIVE any detector returns — a positive can be ~60 bits; under suppression a silence is < 0.1 bit
